items.scenario: You are a third-year student who recently submitted a research proposal for your independent study. Your advisor returned written comments suggesting major revisions to the methodology section. Some feedback contradicts what you understood from earlier meetings, and you need to clarify before continuing.
items.direction: Write an email to Professor Ahmadi. In your email, do the following:
goals: Thank the advisor for the detailed comments on the proposal
Ask which methodology approach the advisor now recommends
Suggest meeting in person to align on the revised timeline
items.subject: Clarifying the methodology direction for my independent study

items.scenario: You recently registered for a six-week ceramics class at the Maplewood Recreation Center. The first session was scheduled for last Tuesday, but the instructor cancelled an hour beforehand citing a kiln malfunction. No make-up date or refund information has been posted.
items.direction: Write an email to Ms. Beaumont. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the situation around the cancelled first session
Ask whether the missed week will be added to the end of the schedule
Request a partial refund if the schedule cannot be extended
items.subject: Schedule and refund question for the ceramics class

items.scenario: Your friend Diego asked you to join a four-day cycling trip along the coastal route during the upcoming break. You want to go but cannot commit to the entire trip because of an unmovable family commitment on the final day. You would like to propose a partial arrangement.
items.direction: Write an email to Diego. In your email, do the following:
goals: Thank Diego for the invitation to the cycling trip
Explain the conflict that prevents joining the full trip
Suggest joining for the first three days and arranging a separate ride back
items.subject: Joining the cycling trip for part of the route

items.scenario: You ordered a custom-printed photo book from an online printing service three weeks ago. The book arrived yesterday with several pages bound out of order and one image cropped incorrectly. The shipment is supposed to be a birthday gift next weekend.
items.direction: Write an email to Mr. Faulkner. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the binding and cropping issues with the photo book
Ask for a corrected copy to be shipped by express delivery
Request confirmation of the new arrival date before the weekend
items.subject: Defective photo book on order PB-30482
